verbGlossary.ALLOCATED BY: {source} distributes schedule buffer or contingency time across activities to absorb risk-driven delays.
verbGlossary.ANALYZED BY: {source} examines financial risk indicators against budget and commitment data to surface cost exposure trends.
verbGlossary.ASSESSED BY: {target} evaluates {source} category for probability and severity based on design documents, site conditions, or trade coordination gaps.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED BY: {source} designates a specific person or trade partner as accountable for monitoring and responding to an identified risk.
verbGlossary.AUDITED BY: {source} conducts formal checks on environmental compliance, verifying permit adherence and site condition conformance.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source} grants formal approval to draw from contingency reserves when {target} event materializes on the project.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {source} compares {target} metric against portfolio history or industry standards to evaluate project safety or cost performance.
verbGlossary.CALCULATED BY: {source} computes {target} score or exposure value using likelihood, impact, and cost data from the project record.
verbGlossary.CALIBRATED BY: {source} adjusts risk matrix thresholds and scoring bands based on project type, contract value, and organizational risk tolerance.
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED BY: {source} assigns risks into structured categories during preconstruction planning to organize {target} register.
verbGlossary.DETECTED BY: {source} identifies {target} condition through model coordination, clash detection, or field observation before it becomes an issue.
verbGlossary.DRAWN DOWN BY: {source} processes contingency expenditures against {target} event, reducing the remaining reserve balance and updating forecasts.
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} verifies that required insurance certificates and coverage limits are current and compliant with contract terms.
verbGlossary.ESCALATED BY: {source} raises a labor or field-level risk to project leadership when conditions exceed crew-level resolution authority.
verbGlossary.EVALUATED BY: {source} scores the likelihood of {target} event occurring based on historical bid data, site conditions, and trade complexity.
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: {source} carries out a mitigation action in the field, implementing corrective work, schedule acceleration, or protective measures.
verbGlossary.FLAGGED BY: {source} marks a coordination or trade-interface risk for review when overlapping scopes or sequencing conflicts are identified.
verbGlossary.FORECASTED BY: {source} projects the schedule impact of identified risks using float analysis, critical path simulation, and resource leveling.
verbGlossary.HEDGED BY: {source} reduces supply chain risk exposure through dual-sourcing, early procurement, or material price lock-in agreements.
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIED BY: {source} recognizes {target} trigger condition in the field based on weather, site access, crew performance, or material delivery status.
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BY: {source} conducts safety inspections on site to identify hazard conditions and verify compliance with the project safety plan.
verbGlossary.MAINTAINED BY: {source} keeps {target} register current throughout the project, updating status, adding new risks, and closing resolved items.
verbGlossary.MITIGATED BY: {source} takes direct field action to reduce construction execution risk through crew resequencing, method changes, or protective work.
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} tracks {target} indicator or residual risk over time, reviewing trends at regular intervals and flagging deviations.
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED BY: {source} establishes risk allocation terms in contracts and subcontracts, defining which party bears specific risk categories.
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {source} converts a qualitative risk assessment into dollar or schedule values using estimating data and project cost models.
verbGlossary.REPORTED BY: {source} documents a safety risk observation or near-miss event, creating the formal record that enters {target} tracking system.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines {target} register or risk metric at set intervals to validate accuracy and confirm response actions are on track.
verbGlossary.SECURED BY: {source} obtains surety bonds from trade partners, verifying bond capacity and confirming coverage against subcontract values.
verbGlossary.SURVEYED BY: {source} investigates geotechnical risk conditions through field observation, test pit results, and soil report correlation.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} follows {target} item or permit condition through its lifecycle, updating status and verifying completion of required actions.
